methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mimic Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company spun out of ETH Zurich, having raised $16M to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—anchored by peers such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation lever. Using total capital raised ($16M) as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $40M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, insufficient data (fewer than 3 comparable companies) could be isolated at matching stage or adjacent stages to generate a reliable median without introducing sector-wide noise.

The analysis triangulates to a median valuation of $40M, with a defensible range of $34M to $46M. This range reflects the single-method limitation inherent in early-stage robotics valuations where stage-based progression dominates over peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly matched Seed-stage comparable transactions and the heterogeneity of application areas within the broader robotics sector. Any refinement would require either additional Seed-stage transaction data within the subsector or clarity on customer traction metrics beyond capital deployed.
